title: Set Up UploadThing File Upload for Next.js
summary: UploadThing file upload integration — route config, upload button/dropzone components, file type validation, and server-side callbacks.
declaration: I will integrate UploadThing file upload into this Next.js project. This includes installing dependencies, creating the file router with type/size validation, setting up the API route, and adding upload UI components. I will NOT skip file type validation or store upload secrets in client code. If you need a different upload solution (S3 direct, Cloudinary, etc.), say so before I begin.
instructions: ## Set Up UploadThing File Upload for Next.js ### 1. Install dependencies - Run `npm install uploadthing @uploadthing/react`. - Verify both packages appear in `package.json` dependencies. - `uploadthing` is the server-side core. `@uploadthing/react` provides React components and hooks. ### 2. Environment variables - Check if `.env.local` exists. If not, create it (but NEVER commit it). - Ensure `.env.local` is listed in `.gitignore`. - Add the following variables: ``` UPLOADTHING_TOKEN=... ``` - Get the token from the UploadThing dashboard (uploadthing.com) > Project > API Keys. - The `UPLOADTHING_TOKEN` contains both the app ID and secret — a single variable is all that is needed. ### 3. Create the file router - Create `lib/uploadthing.ts` (or `src/lib/uploadthing.ts` if using `src/` directory). - Import `createUploadthing` and `type FileRouter` from `uploadthing/next`. - Define your file routes. Each route specifies: allowed file types, max file size, max file count, and middleware/callback. - Example routes to create: - `imageUploader`: accepts `image` up to 4MB, max 4 files. - `documentUploader`: accepts `pdf` up to 16MB, max 1 file. - In the `middleware` function: authenticate the user (check session/token), return metadata (e.g., userId). If the user is not authorized, throw an `UploadThingError`. - In the `onUploadComplete` callback: receive the metadata and file info. Log or save the file URL to your database here. - Export the file router type: `export type OurFileRouter = typeof ourFileRouter`. ### 4. Create the API route - Create `app/api/uploadthing/route.ts`. - Import `createRouteHandler` from `uploadthing/next`. - Import your file router from `@/lib/uploadthing`. - Call `createRouteHandler({ router: ourFileRouter })` and export the returned `GET` and `POST` handlers. - This is the entire API route file — UploadThing handles all upload logic internally. ### 5. Generate typed React components - Create `lib/uploadthing-components.ts`. - Import `generateUploadButton`, `generateUploadDropzone`, and `generateUploader` from `@uploadthing/react`. - Import your `OurFileRouter` type. - Generate and export typed components: - `export const UploadButton = generateUploadButton()` - `export const UploadDropzone = generateUploadDropzone()` - `export const Uploader = generateUploader()` - These components are type-safe — they know your route names and accepted file types. ### 6. Add UploadThing styles - Import UploadThing CSS in your root layout or global CSS file. - Add `import '@uploadthing/react/styles.css'` to `app/layout.tsx` or add `@import '@uploadthing/react/styles.css'` to your global CSS. - If using Tailwind and want to customize, you can skip the default CSS and use the `appearance` prop on components instead. ### 7. Use upload components in pages - Create a page or component that uses the upload button. - Import `UploadButton` or `UploadDropzone` from your generated components file. - Mark the component with `'use client'` — upload components are client-side interactive. - Use the `endpoint` prop to specify which file route to use: ``. - Handle callbacks: - `onClientUploadComplete`: fires when upload succeeds. Receives an array of uploaded file objects with `url`, `name`, `size`, and `key`. - `onUploadError`: fires on failure. Display the error message to the user. - `onUploadBegin`: fires when upload starts. Use for loading states. ### 8. Access uploaded files - After upload, `onClientUploadComplete` provides file URLs in the format `https://utfs.io/f/{fileKey}`. - Store these URLs in your database, associated with the user/entity. - To display uploaded images, use `` or Next.js `` with the UploadThing URL. - If using Next.js ``, add `utfs.io` to the `images.remotePatterns` in `next.config.js`: ``` images: { remotePatterns: [{ protocol: 'https', hostname: 'utfs.io' }] } ``` ### 9. Add file deletion support - Import `UTApi` from `uploadthing/server`. - Create a server action or API route for deletion. - Instantiate: `const utapi = new UTApi()`. - Delete by file key: `await utapi.deleteFiles(fileKey)` or multiple: `await utapi.deleteFiles([key1, key2])`. - Always authenticate the user before allowing deletion — verify they own the file. ### 10. Verify the setup - Start the dev server: `npm run dev`. - Navigate to the page with the upload component. - Upload a test image — confirm it succeeds and the URL is returned in `onClientUploadComplete`. - Check the UploadThing dashboard — confirm the file appears. - Test uploading a disallowed file type — confirm it is rejected. - Test uploading a file exceeding the size limit — confirm it is rejected. - Check the server logs — confirm the `onUploadComplete` callback fires with correct metadata. ### Common Errors & Fixes - **"UPLOADTHING_TOKEN is not set"** — You are missing the env var. Copy it from the UploadThing dashboard. Restart the dev server after adding it. - **Upload fails with 401 or 403** — Your middleware function is throwing (likely auth check failing). For development, temporarily return an empty object `{}` from middleware to test without auth. - **"Failed to fetch" in browser console** — The API route is not set up correctly. Verify `app/api/uploadthing/route.ts` exports both GET and POST. - **File type rejected despite being allowed** — Check the file route config. File type names in UploadThing are: `image`, `video`, `audio`, `pdf`, `text`, `blob` (for any). They are NOT MIME types. - **Next.js Image component shows 404** — You did not add `utfs.io` to `next.config.js` remote patterns. Add it and restart the dev server. - **Styles not loading** — Ensure you imported `@uploadthing/react/styles.css`. If using Tailwind, ensure the CSS import comes before Tailwind directives. - **onUploadComplete not firing on server** — Check server logs for errors in your callback. If you throw inside `onUploadComplete`, the upload still succeeds (file is stored) but the callback fails silently. ### What NOT to Do - Do NOT expose `UPLOADTHING_TOKEN` to the client — it is a server-side secret. Only the generated React components communicate with the API route, which uses the token server-side. - Do NOT skip file type and size validation — UploadThing enforces these on the server, but defining them explicitly catches errors early and provides better UX. - Do NOT store file URLs without associating them with a user — orphaned files are hard to clean up and cost storage. - Do NOT use UploadThing components in Server Components — they must be in `'use client'` components. - Do NOT hardcode file keys or URLs — they may change if files are re-uploaded or deleted. Always store the URL returned from the upload callback. - Do NOT skip authentication in the middleware for production — without it, anyone can upload files to your account.
